title: Citrusy British Bacon Grill
description: A crispy and flavorful brunch dish that combines the richness of low-fat British bacon with the brightness of lime.

ingredients.name: Low-fat British bacon
ingredients.notes: Choose a thick-cut, low-fat bacon for the crispiest results.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed is best, but bottled is fine too.

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Adds a touch of sweetness to balance out the acidity.

ingredients.name: English mustard
ingredients.notes: Classic combination with bacon and lime.

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Granulated garlic
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground for the best flavor.

instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, about 400°F (200°C).
In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, brown sugar, English mustard, and granulated garlic (if using).
Add the sliced British bacon to the marinade and toss to coat. Let it sit for at least 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, toast the brioche buns on the grill or in a toaster.
Brush the grill grates with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Remove the bacon from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Grill the bacon slices for 2-3 minutes per side, or until crispy and golden.
Transfer the cooked bacon to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess fat.
Wipe the grill clean with a paper towel to remove any remaining bacon bits.
Brush the grill grates with olive oil again to prevent sticking.
Grill the lime zest for 30 seconds to 1 minute, or until fragrant and lightly charred.
To assemble the Citrusy British Bacon Grill, place a few slices of bacon on each toasted bun, followed by a sprinkle of chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
